mnemonics: V.I.P. C.A.R. - Verifying Is Part of Creating A Reliable (design)
S.S.S. - Start with Specs, Synthesize, Simulate
keyPoints: Specification: Define the problem and what the circuit must do.
Synthesis: Translate the specification into a logical diagram or HDL code.
Verification: Use simulation and timing analysis to ensure the design meets the specification and functions correctly.
commonMistakes: Skipping the specification phase and starting to design immediately, leading to a circuit that solves the wrong problem.
Not performing adequate verification through simulation, resulting in a design with hidden logical or timing errors.
